candidate_direction: Model 7 versus Model 8: intervention-related post-intervention trajectory differences beyond group mean-structure differences
candidate_note: Recomputed from the author's lavaan latent-basis models using p_tot1-p_tot5 and opt_in. Model 7 is lb_fit3: lb_growth_par with group-specific means and post-intervention loadings, under the parallel pre-intervention loading assumption. Model 8 is lb_fit4: lb_growth with group-specific mean structure but loadings constrained equal across opt-in groups. lavaan::lavTestLRT(lb_fit3, lb_fit4) gave Model 7 chi-square = 25.579863, df = 23; Model 8 chi-square = 35.889584, df = 25; chi-square difference = 10.309722, df difference = 2, p = 0.00577128. n_total = 370; opt-out n = 180; opt-in n = 190.

candidate_project_result: Learning condition x question type interaction: F(1, 64) = 5.18, p = .026, eta_p2 = .08
candidate_direction: Testing effect for reviewed/practiced content is larger than the testing-minus-restudy difference for unreviewed lecture content.
candidate_note: Recomputed from the author's SPSS GLM target using complete cases on the four criterion-score variables. The target within-subject interaction was reconstructed as the contrast (Testing_old - Testing_New) - (Restudy_old - Restudy_New), then tested as the intercept in lm(contrast ~ Lecture + questiontype_crit) using sum contrasts, matching the SPSS repeated-measures GLM with Lecture and questiontype_crit as between-subject factors and no Lecture x questiontype_crit term. n_total = 67; Lecture counts: 1=39; 2=28; questiontype_crit counts: -1=37; 1=30. Target interaction: F(1, 64) = 5.183127, p = 0.0261602, eta_p2 = 0.074919. Follow-up reviewed/tested content contrast: F(1, 64) = 5.325433, p = 0.0242607, eta_p2 = 0.076818. Follow-up unreviewed-content contrast: F(1, 64) = 0.739777, p = 0.392943, eta_p2 = 0.011427. Question-type main effect: F(1, 64) = 19.091631, p = 4.66226e-05, eta_p2 = 0.229766. Learning-condition main effect: F(1, 64) = 0.634395, p = 0.428692, eta_p2 = 0.009815.

candidate_direction: Experiment 1 final test performance differs across one quiz, two quizzes, three quizzes, and mastery quizzing
candidate_note: Recomputed from the author's Experiment 1 cleaned data file with failed-mastery participants and timed-out participants removed. The analysis uses only Condition and Final Test Score; additional summary columns in the CSV are ignored. Welch's one-way ANOVA gives F(3, 79.568551) = 35.642056, p = 1.05266e-14. Omega squared is computed from the ordinary one-way ANOVA sums of squares, matching the article's reported omega2. Group summaries: one: n=38, M=7.447368, SD=1.703690; two: n=40, M=6.575000, SD=1.795828; three: n=38, M=7.105263, SD=2.251600; mastery: n=38, M=9.473684, SD=1.006381. Reported BF10 = 2.06e8 is retained as article metadata but is not recomputed here.

candidate_direction: identity-safety-cue syllabus versus control syllabus on expected social-psychology field belonging
candidate_note: Recomputed from the author SPSS syntax: fieldexp_4 was reverse-coded as 6-fieldexp_4, the nine field belonging items were averaged, 3-SD outliers were winsorized to the nearest non-outlier value, and fieldbelong_high was analysed with UNIANOVA BY IV_profgender IV_safetycues /METHOD=SSTYPE(3) /DESIGN=IV_profgender IV_safetycues IV_profgender*IV_safetycues. Result: F(1, 322) = 5.169120, p = 0.02365, eta_p2 = 0.015800. Control n = 163, safety n = 163; control M = 3.472393, safety M = 3.593047.
